description: Standalone watchOS SwiftUI app that converts HealthKit data (steps, active calories, sleep, exercise minutes) into four energy types that hatch, grow, and evolve a Digimon through 7 stages. Dominant energy type determines the evolution branch. Includes feeding, training, battles, sickness, death and rebirth. Sprites are 48x64 PNGs = 3x4 grid of 16x16 frames (12 frames: walk1, walk2, eat1, eat2, sleep1, sleep2, refuse, happy, angry, hurt1, hurt2, attack). Source PRD: tasks/prd-digimon-health-vpet.md

userStories.title: Create watchOS project via XcodeGen spec
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a standalone watchOS app target defined as a text spec so the project can be generated and built reproducibly.
acceptanceCriteria: Install xcodegen if missing (brew install xcodegen)
Create project.yml defining a standalone watchOS app target named DigiVPet, SwiftUI lifecycle, no iOS companion target
Deployment target set to watchOS 10.0
Info.plist includes NSHealthShareUsageDescription with a reason string
Running 'xcodegen generate' produces DigiVPet.xcodeproj with no errors
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)
userStories.notes: project.yml defines a single watchOS app target DigiVPet (SwiftUI @main App, no iOS companion, TARGETED_DEVICE_FAMILY=4, WKApplication=true for standalone). Verified: xcodegen 2.46.0 already installed; 'xcodegen generate' creates DigiVPet.xcodeproj with no errors; WATCHOS_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET=10.0; built Info.plist (Support/Info.plist, GENERATE_INFOPLIST_FILE=NO) contains NSHealthShareUsageDescription; clean build for Series 11 (46mm) BUILD SUCCEEDED with zero source warnings. Sources/ holds DigiVPetApp.swift + placeholder ContentView.swift. No test target yet — US-002 is the first story that needs one and should add it to project.yml.

userStories.title: Bundle sprite folder and add SpriteLoader
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the sprite sheets bundled as a folder reference so they can be loaded by path at runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: Add '16x16 Digimon Sprites' to the target as a folder reference (NOT an asset catalog), preserving stage subfolders
sprites_cut/ is NOT bundled — it is generated verification output, not a shipped asset
SpriteLoader.loadSheet(stage:name:) returns a non-nil CGImage for stage 'Child', name 'Agumon'
SpriteLoader returns nil (never crashes) for a missing file
Unit test asserts Child/Agumon loads at 48x64 and a bogus name returns nil
Tests pass
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)
userStories.notes: '16x16 Digimon Sprites' added to project.yml as type: folder (folder reference, buildPhase: resources) — verified in the built .app: stage subfolders preserved, no Assets.car, sprites_cut absent. Sources/SpriteLoader.swift exposes url(stage:name:in:) and loadSheet(stage:name:in:) (bundle injectable, defaults to .main); decodes via CGImageSourceCreateWithURL, returns nil for missing/undecodable. Added the first test target DigiVPetTests (bundle.unit-test, TEST_HOST/BUNDLE_LOADER on DigiVPet, XCTest not Swift Testing) + scheme testTargets. 6/6 tests pass, BUILD SUCCEEDED. TWO REAL BUGS FOUND AND FIXED: (1) Info.plist needed WKWatchOnly: true — WKApplication alone builds but FAILS TO INSTALL ('must specify WKCompanionAppBundleIdentifier'), which US-001 could not catch since it only ran build, never install; any story that installs/tests depends on this. (2) Bundle.url(forResource:) treats an EMPTY name like nil and returns an ARBITRARY png from the directory (stage Child yielded Morphomon), so loadSheet now guards !name.isEmpty && !stage.isEmpty — an empty spriteFile must fail loudly, relevant to the US-009 validator.

userStories.title: Sprite frame verification tool
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to export frames as individual PNGs so I can visually confirm the slicing math and frame labels are correct.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/cut_sprites.swift slices a 48x64 sheet into 12 16x16 PNGs, row-major, using x = (i % 3) * 16, y = (i / 3) * 16 — the SAME math the app uses at runtime
Digitama sheets (48x16) are handled as 3 frames named idle, wobble, hatch (frame 3 is the egg cracking open)
Script rejects any sheet that is not 48x64 or 48x16 rather than emitting garbage frames
'swift scripts/cut_sprites.swift --demo' cuts the 3 seed lines (Agumon, Gabumon, Palmon): 21 sheets, 225 frames, zero failures
Frame content verified against its label (03_eat1 open mouth, 05_sleep1 closed eyes)
Output sprites_cut/ is gitignored generated output and is NOT bundled into the app
userStories.notes: DONE (pre-Ralph, 2026-07-17). This is a DEV TOOL ONLY — the app slices at runtime; it does not ship cut frames. Benchmarked 2026-07-17 over 216 frames x50: runtime slicing 1.26ms vs pre-cut 18.87ms decode-only (15x), 4.41ms vs 27.48ms realized (6.2x); 18 file opens vs 216; full roster 1.2MB/865 files vs 4.6MB/10,380. PNG per-file decode overhead dominates at 16x16, and cropping(to:) shares the sheet's backing buffer so it is near-free. The tool earned its keep: cutting the frames is what visually confirmed the xlsx frame order matches the art, and revealed Digitama frame 3 is the hatch.

userStories.title: Add SpriteFrame enum and runtime sheet slicing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to slice a 48x64 sheet into its 12 frames at runtime so frames cost one decode instead of twelve.
acceptanceCriteria: SpriteFrame enum with 12 cases mapped to indices 0-11: walk1=0, walk2=1, eat1=2, eat2=3, sleep1=4, sleep2=5, refuse=6, happy=7, angry=8, hurt1=9, hurt2=10, attack=11
Slicing uses CGImage.cropping(to:) with x = (index % 3) * 16, y = (index / 3) * 16, size 16x16
The sheet is decoded ONCE per Digimon and the 12 cropped CGImages are cached in an array — never re-crop per animation tick, and never re-decode the sheet per frame
Handles 48x16 Digitama sheets as 3 frames (idle, wobble, hatch); requesting .attack on an egg returns nil rather than crashing
Unit test asserts frame 11 (attack) slices to rect (32, 48, 16, 16)
Unit test asserts a 48x16 Digitama yields exactly 3 frames
Unit test asserts loading a Digimon twice decodes the sheet only once (cache is hit)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)
userStories.notes: Sources/SpriteSheet.swift adds SpriteFrame (Int-raw, walk1=0...attack=11), EggFrame (idle/wobble/hatch), SpriteSheet (crops a decoded sheet via cropping(to:) using x=(i%3)*16, y=(i/3)*16) and SpriteSheetCache (decode-once, injectable decoder, NSLock). Observed: BUILD SUCCEEDED, TEST SUCCEEDED 18/18 (12 new), zero source warnings. Verified attack.sourceRect == (32,48,16,16); Child/Agumon -> 12 frames of 16x16; Digitama/Agu_Digitama -> exactly 3; counting decoder proves 3 sheet() calls = 1 decode and the second call returns the IDENTICAL CGImage objects (=== per frame), so frames are never re-cropped. DESIGN NOTE for US-005: EggFrame and SpriteFrame are SEPARATE enums and each subscript returns nil for the other kind of sheet — egg index 2 is the hatch where a stage sheet's index 2 is eat1, so one shared enum would let sheet[.eat1] silently return the hatch. Consequence: sheet[SpriteFrame.walk1] on a Digitama is nil, so DigimonSpriteView must drive eggs with EggFrame (idle->wobble) and must not fall back to walk frames. Sheets that are not 48x64/48x16 return nil from SpriteSheet(sheet:) rather than garbage frames. Failed loads are cached too, so a missing sprite does not re-hit the disk every animation tick.

userStories.title: Add DigimonSpriteView with animation loops
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable view that renders and animates sprite frames so every screen can show a Digimon.
acceptanceCriteria: DigimonSpriteView renders a given SpriteFrame scaled up with .interpolation(.none)
Supports named loops: idle (walk1 to walk2), eat (eat1 to eat2), sleep (sleep1 to sleep2), hurt (hurt1 to hurt2)
Frame duration is 500ms and defined in one constant
Renders a placeholder view when the sprite is missing
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)
Verify in watchOS Simulator (xcrun simctl io booted screenshot)
userStories.notes: Sources/DigimonSpriteView.swift adds SpriteAnimation (idle/eat/sleep/hurt + still(SpriteFrame)) and DigimonSpriteView. Observed: BUILD SUCCEEDED zero source warnings, TEST SUCCEEDED 26/26 (8 new), and the app installed+launched on Series 11 (46mm). Frame duration is the single constant SpriteAnimation.frameDuration = 0.5. VERIFIED IN SIM, not just by build: 6 screenshots 250ms apart alternate between EXACTLY 2 distinct md5s with a 500ms period — the loop genuinely advances at 500ms. Pixel-compared the two shots: big Agumon's legs change (walk1->walk2), the egg goes squat->rounded (idle->wobble), eat and sleep both advance, while still(.attack) and the missing-sprite '?' placeholder stay byte-identical. Pixels are hard-edged at 4x scale = .interpolation(.none) is really on. DESIGN: a pose is a 1-frame loop (case still), so US-024/025/028/031 render refuse/attack/angry/happy via .still(.refuse) etc. and get no TimelineView (a held pose must not redraw forever). Eggs follow the US-004 note: SpriteAnimation.idle maps to EggFrame idle->wobble via eggFrames, and eat/sleep/hurt/still have NO egg art (return []) so they show the placeholder rather than borrowing walk indices, which on a 48x16 sheet would land on the HATCH. frameIndex(at:count:) is a pure function of wall-clock time (not a stored counter) so a view rebuilt mid-loop resumes where the animation is; it is unit-tested at 0/0.49/0.5/0.99/1.0/1.5s with no real waiting, and guards count<=1 (no divide-by-zero) and pre-2001 dates (a bare % would return a negative index and crash the lookup). ContentView is now a temporary DigimonSpriteView demo harness — US-016 replaces it with the real main screen.

userStories.title: Add SwiftData persistence model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need game state saved on-device so the Digimon survives app restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: SwiftData model GameState with: currentDigimonId, stage, stageEnergy (4 values), lifetimeEnergy (4 values), birthDate, stageEnteredDate, careMistakeCount, hunger, strengthStat, healthStatus, battleWins, battleLosses
Model persists and reloads across app relaunch
resetGame() clears state back to a new Digitama
Unit test asserts save then load round-trips all fields
Tests pass
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)
userStories.notes: Sources/GameState.swift adds @Model GameState with all 12 fields, plus EnergyType, Stage, HealthStatus and an EnergyTotals struct (4 values, subscriptable by EnergyType so callers need no switch). Sources/GameStore.swift owns the SwiftData stack: loadOrCreate(digitamaId:now:), resetGame(digitamaId:now:) and save(); the store URL is injectable so tests get a clean temp file. Observed: BUILD SUCCEEDED zero source warnings, TEST SUCCEEDED 37/37 (11 new). Round-trip test writes DISTINCT values to every field (no two fields share a value, so a crossed wire cannot round-trip by coincidence), drops the container, and reads through a brand new one pointed at the same file. RELAUNCH VERIFIED LITERALLY, not by proxy: read the app's real default.store back with sqlite3 AFTER the test process exited and found ZGAMESTATE = agu_digitama / Digitama / birthDate 700000 (== t0) — the data outlived the process that wrote it, which is what surviving a relaunch means. That also confirms Stage/HealthStatus persist as their STRING raw values ('Digitama', 'healthy'), so renaming a case rewrites saved games — change displayName instead. NOTE FOR LATER STORIES: every other test passes a temp URL, so none of them touch the store the shipping app opens; testDefaultStoreLocationOpensAndPersists exists specifically to cover GameStore() at SwiftData's default location, which no other test would catch failing. Opening it logs alarming CoreData noise ('Failed to stat path', 'Sandbox access to file-write-create denied') — this is a FALSE ALARM: SwiftData probes Library/Application Support before creating it, then succeeds. The file and its -wal are real on disk; do not go bug-hunting on those log lines. resetGame is a TOTAL wipe (lifetimeEnergy and the battle record included) — US-029 rebirth after death must NOT route through it, since lifetimeEnergy and the Dex are required to outlive a Digimon. Stage raw values ARE the sprite subfolder names (all 8 asserted to be real directories), so DigimonSpriteView(stage: state.stage.rawValue) needs no mapping table; Armor-Hybrid has ladderIndex nil on purpose so the US-009 stage-skip check treats it as unknown rather than the rung after Ultimate.

userStories.title: Define evolutions.json schema and Swift models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the evolution tree as external data so the roster extends without code changes.
acceptanceCriteria: evolutions.json schema documented with node fields: id, displayName, stage, spriteFile, variant, evolutions[]
Each edge carries: to, requiredEnergy (strength|vitality|spirit|stamina), minEnergy, maxCareMistakes, optional minBattleWins, optional isDefault
Schema supports multiple edges per node (branching) and multiple parents per node (converging)
Codable Swift models decode the file at launch
Unit test decodes a small fixture with a branching node and asserts both edges parse
Tests pass
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)
userStories.notes: Sources/EvolutionGraph.swift adds EvolutionEdge, EvolutionNode and EvolutionGraph (all Codable), plus load(from:) and a `bundled` static decoded once. Schema documented in docs/evolutions-schema.md (not bundled; only Resources/ is). Resources/evolutions.json added to project.yml as buildPhase: resources — it lands at the BUNDLE ROOT, so Bundle.url(forResource:"evolutions", withExtension:"json") finds it with no subdirectory. Observed: BUILD SUCCEEDED zero source warnings, TEST SUCCEEDED 51/51 (14 new). DECODE AT LAUNCH VERIFIED IN SIM, not just by test: DigiVPetApp.init() touches EvolutionGraph.bundled and the temporary ContentView demo prints the node count — a screenshot on Series 11 (46mm) shows 'graph: 4 nodes'. The bundled-file test is proven NON-VACUOUS by mutation: setting requiredEnergy to 'wisdom' in the real evolutions.json turned the suite red, so the test really reads the shipped file. DEVIATION FROM THE AC, FLAGGED FOR REVIEW: requiredEnergy is OPTIONAL in the model, where the AC implies it is required (it marks only minBattleWins/isDefault optional). Nil is legal on exactly one kind of edge — a Digitama's hatch edge — because US-018 hatches on TOTAL energy across all four types, so no single type gates it and naming one would be data that lies, which a later reader would eventually 'fix' the engine to respect and break hatching. Every edge out of a hatched node carries it, in both the seed and the fixture; US-009's validator should enforce 'requiredEnergy present unless stage == Digitama'. Also added dexOnly (optional, default false) though the AC does not list it: US-010 must emit it, and US-009 cannot otherwise tell 'missing sheet' from 'one of the 157 idle-only Digimon' when checking spriteFile exists. Its open question is left to US-010: a dexOnly node's art is in the FLAT 'Idle Frame Only/' folder, so its true stage is NOT derivable from disk and its spriteFile does not resolve under the stage folder. Seed holds one partial line only (Agu_Digitama -> Botamon -> Koromon -> Agumon, terminal at Agumon, all four verified on disk) — US-008 owns the three full lines and the branching node. TRAP FOR THE NEXT ITERATION: a graph that fails to DECODE crashes the TEST HOST at launch (the app is TEST_HOST and `bundled` traps), so xcodebuild reports 'Early unexpected exit ... test runner crashed before establishing connection' and NO test reports — it names neither the file nor the graph. The real diagnostic is above it in the log: grep 'Could not load the evolution graph', which gives the exact JSON path e.g. nodes[1].evolutions[0].requiredEnergy.

userStories.title: Seed evolution data for three complete lines
userStories.description: As a developer, I need real seed data so the whole loop can be exercised end-to-end before the full roster exists.
acceptanceCriteria: evolutions.json contains 3 complete lines, each with Digitama, Baby I, Baby II, Child, Adult, Perfect, Ultimate
Line 1: Agumon to Greymon to MetalGreymon to WarGreymon
Line 2: Gabumon to Garurumon to WereGarurumon to MetalGarurumon
Line 3: Palmon to Togemon to Lilimon to Rosemon (Baby forms Yuramon, Tanemon)
Every node's spriteFile matches a real file that exists on disk under '16x16 Digimon Sprites'

Every node uses a 48x64 animated sheet from a stage folder — never an 'Idle Frame Only' sprite, since 157 of those have no animated sheet (e.g. Poyomon), which is why the Patamon line is NOT usable as seed data
At least one node has 2+ outgoing edges with different requiredEnergy values (branching is exercised)
Every non-terminal node has exactly one edge marked isDefault
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)
userStories.notes: Resources/evolutions.json now holds 22 nodes: the 3 required lines complete from Digitama through Ultimate (Agu_Digitama->Botamon->Koromon->Agumon->Greymon->MetalGreymon->WarGreymon; Gabu_Digitama->Punimon->Tsunomon->Gabumon->Garurumon->WereGarurumon->MetalGarurumon; Pal_Digitama->Yuramon->Tanemon->Palmon->Togemon->Lilimon->Rosemon), plus Meramon (Adult) as the branch target. Observed: BUILD SUCCEEDED zero source warnings, TEST SUCCEEDED 59/59 (8 new in Tests/SeedRosterTests.swift, which tests the SHIPPED data — EvolutionGraphTests keeps owning the model via fixture). All 21 seed sprites `ls`+sips-verified before authoring: Digitama 48x16, the other 18 48x64, all under stage folders, none from 'Idle Frame Only'. testEverySeedNodeHasAnAnimatedSheetWithTheRightFrameCount slices every node the way the app does, so 'has an animated sheet' means 12 real frames (3 for eggs), not merely 'file exists'. BRANCHING: Agumon -> Greymon (strength, isDefault) | Meramon (stamina), and Meramon converges back to MetalGreymon so the branch is a detour, not a dead end (parents(of: metalgreymon) == [greymon, meramon]). Energy assignment uses all four types: line 1 strength, line 2 vitality, line 3 spirit, stamina on the Agumon->Meramon branch. TESTS PROVEN NON-VACUOUS BY MUTATION (3 mutations of the real file, each caught by exactly the right test, file restored byte-identical after): botamon spriteFile -> Poyomon (an idle-only Digimon with no sheet in Baby I/) turned the sheet test red; deleting greymon's isDefault turned the default-edge AND both line-completeness tests red; collapsing the Agumon branch to strength/strength turned both branching tests red. THAT MUTATION FOUND A REAL BUG IN MY OWN TEST: testAgumonBranchesOnStrengthOrStamina keyed a Dictionary(uniqueKeysWithValues:) on requiredEnergy, which TRAPPED on the duplicate key ('Fatal error: Duplicate values for key: strength') and killed the TEST HOST, so only 33 of 59 tests ran — the exact failure mode US-007 warns about. Rewritten to first(where:) + a Set-count assertion; re-ran the same mutation and confirmed 0 crashes, all 59 tests run, and it now FAILS cleanly. DELIBERATELY OMITTED: no seed edge sets minBattleWins. On a node whose only edge is isDefault, US-020's fallback fires that edge after the time gate regardless of whether it qualifies, so a minBattleWins there would be data that lies (same reasoning as US-007's requiredEnergy note); US-019 should cover it by fixture. For the same reason every threshold on a single-edge node is advisory — only the Agumon branch has data that actually decides an outcome. maxCareMistakes is graded 5/4/3/2/2 up the ladder; the Digitama hatch edges keep minEnergy 50 + maxCareMistakes 99 so neglect can never block hatching.

userStories.title: Add evolution graph validator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the graph validated at build time so a broken edge fails the tests instead of the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Validator reports edges whose 'to' references an unknown node id
Validator reports nodes whose spriteFile does not exist on disk
Validator reports stage transitions that skip a stage
Validator reports non-terminal nodes with no isDefault edge
Validator runs as a unit test against the real evolutions.json and fails the build on any error
Tests pass
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)
userStories.notes: Sources/EvolutionGraphValidator.swift adds GraphValidationError (11 cases, CustomStringConvertible) and EvolutionGraph.validate(spriteExists:), which returns ALL errors in node order rather than throwing on the first — fixing a roster one error per run would be miserable and the errors are independent. Observed: BUILD SUCCEEDED zero source warnings, TEST SUCCEEDED 78/78 (19 new in Tests/EvolutionGraphValidatorTests.swift). The sprite check is the one rule that touches the world, so it is injected (typealias SpriteExistsCheck); fixture tests stub it TRUE so the other rules cannot fail on absent art, while the missing-sprite tests use the real EvolutionGraph.spriteExists(in:) against the bundle. THE AC TEST IS PROVEN NON-VACUOUS BY MUTATION — testShippedEvolutionsJsonIsValid runs the validator on the real evolutions.json, and all four required checks were made to FAIL on purpose against that file (restored byte-identical after, md5 06d2488 verified): (1) greymon->nosuchmon => 'no node has id nosuchmon'; (2) agumon spriteFile->NotADigimon => 'does not exist on disk (Child/NotADigimon.png)'; (3) greymon stage->Perfect => 'agumon -> greymon: Child to Perfect does not advance exactly one stage' (and caught the resulting Perfect/Greymon.png too, showing errors accumulate); (4) delete greymon isDefault => 'non-terminal node has no isDefault edge'. Each mutation used a VALID enum string so it stayed a SEMANTIC error the validator can see — per US-007's trap, a bad enum/syntax kills the TEST_HOST before any test reports, so the validator can never be what catches it. Also added testTheValidatedGraphIsTheRealNonEmptyRoster (asserts 22 nodes + Agumon present), because validating an empty graph would trivially find no errors, and testASoundGraphHasNoErrors as the control so 'exactly one error' assertions cannot pass on unrelated noise. Stage-skip uses Stage.ladderIndex and SKIPS nil (Armor-Hybrid) rather than treating it as the rung after Ultimate, per the US-006 note — testDoesNotReportTransitionsInvolvingArmorHybrid pins that; it reports sideways/backwards edges too, since the same rung arithmetic catches them and they are equally broken. BEYOND THE AC, all four explicitly handed to US-009 by earlier stories: requiredEnergy present unless stage==Digitama AND absent when it IS (US-007), duplicate ids (found against `nodes`, not `byId`, which keeps only the first and so cannot see them), empty spriteFile as its OWN error rather than left to a nil load (US-002: Bundle.url treats an empty name like nil and returns an ARBITRARY png, so a check that does not guard would 'find' art and pass — the test stubs existence TRUE to prove the rule does not lean on the loader guarding), and edges naming a dexOnly node. Added SpriteLoader.idleFrameOnlyFolder: a dexOnly node's art is in the FLAT 'Idle Frame Only' folder, so resolving it under its stage folder would report all 157 as missing — verified on disk that 'Baby I/Poyomon.png' does NOT exist while 'Idle Frame Only/Poyomon.png' does, and testDexOnlyNodeResolvesItsSpriteInIdleFrameOnly asserts both directions. That answers half of US-007's open question (where dexOnly art resolves); the other half stands — a dexOnly node's true stage is still NOT derivable from disk, so US-010 must source it elsewhere. Also reports multipleDefaultEdges: two fallbacks make US-020's choice depend on edge order.

userStories.title: Add roster import script
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a script that generates node boilerplate from sprite filenames so 2270 nodes are not hand-typed.
acceptanceCriteria: Script (scripts/import_roster.py) walks the sprite folders and emits a JSON node per sprite with id, displayName, stage, spriteFile
Parses variant suffixes (_X, _Black, _Blue, _Virus, _2006, _2010, _YnK) into a 'variant' field
Strips stage-disambiguating suffixes (_Child, _Adult) from displayName
Re-running the script merges with existing hand-authored evolutions[] edges without clobbering them
Script prints a summary count per stage and a list of Digitama it could not match to a Child form
Script flags Digimon that exist only in 'Idle Frame Only' with no animated stage sheet (157 expected) and marks them dexOnly: true so the evolution engine never selects them as playable
Script documented in README.md
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)
userStories.notes: scripts/import_roster.py walks the sprite tree and emits all 1022 nodes with id, displayName, stage, spriteFile, variant and dexOnly derived from disk. Observed: 865 playable + 157 dexOnly = 1022, which reproduces the PRD's verified counts exactly (per-stage 57/43/47/120/192/169/221/16). BUILD SUCCEEDED zero source warnings; the existing 78 tests still pass untouched (no Swift changed). README.md created (the repo had none) and documents the script; docs/evolutions-schema.md updated so it no longer contradicts it. Cross-checked the generated file against every applicable US-009 rule in Python: 0 missing sprites, 0 duplicate ids, 0 empty spriteFiles, 0 edges to unknown/dexOnly ids, 0 playable nodes lacking a stage, 0 ids off the lowercased-basename convention. All 7 required variant suffixes verified to FIRE on real files (X 112, Black 14, Blue 3, Virus 3, 2006 4, 2010 2, YnK 1; 137 nodes carry a variant, 0 whose variant tokens are not literally in the filename) rather than spot-checked. MERGE PROVEN NON-VACUOUS: re-running in place is byte-identical (md5 twice); hand-authoring a NEW edge (wargreymon -> omegamon) and a NEW dexOnly stage (ankylomon -> Adult) then re-running preserved BOTH; and the control (running with --graph pointed at a nonexistent file) yields 0 nodes with edges, proving the 19 preserved edge-sets come from the merge and not from the generator. All 22 seed nodes' evolutions[] are carried over byte-identical. DELIBERATE: --out defaults to roster.generated.json (gitignored), NOT the shipped graph. Resources/evolutions.json is untouched (git diff empty) because it is CURATED: regenerating it wholesale would swap 3 playable lines for ~1000 terminal nodes and leave 54 of 57 Digitama with no hatch edge, breaking US-018's random-egg start. The PRD says to build the loop on 3-4 lines then grow the data. THE dexOnly STAGE GAP IS NOW ANSWERED, and the answer is that it is NOT derivable: US-009 handed US-010 'a dexOnly node's true stage must be sourced from somewhere other than the folder tree'. I checked every candidate source and there is none. 'Idle Frame Only/' is flat; 'Black and White Sprites/' has stage subfolders but covers only 1 of the 157 (Bulkmon); LCD Checklist.xlsx is per-device with NO stage/level column at all (confirmed by parsing its sharedStrings: 0 cells equal Child/Adult/Perfect/Ultimate/Level/Stage -- it is a frame checklist, its columns are Walk 1..Attack, matching the PRD); Visual Checklist.png is an unlabeled completion collage. Only 5 name their own stage (Arkadimon_Adult/_Perfect/_Ultimate/_SuperUltimate, Meicoo_Child) and those are derived and believed; Arkadimon_Baby is left unknown because '_Baby' maps to both Baby I and Baby II (Arkadimon_Baby the animated sprite is filed under Baby II). The other 152 are emitted as "stage": null meaning UNKNOWN. I did NOT invent stages from Digimon knowledge -- unverifiable data a later reader would trust is exactly the failure mode US-002/US-007/US-008 kept catching. OPEN DECISION LEFT FOR A LATER STORY, NOT HIDDEN: stage:null is a GENERATOR convention that Sources/EvolutionGraph.swift does NOT accept -- EvolutionNode.stage is a non-optional Stage, so a null-stage node fails the decode and EvolutionGraph.bundled would trap at launch. Nothing is broken today (the generated file is not bundled, and no shipped node is dexOnly), but a dexOnly node needs a stage assigned before being promoted into Resources/evolutions.json -- and re-runs preserve what is assigned, which is why the merge harvests a dexOnly stage as well as edges. The alternative is making stage optional (Stage? = 'unknown', legal only when dexOnly) plus a validator rule; I left it out as out of scope for this story and it ripples into SeedRosterTests/EvolutionGraphTests, which read node.stage.rawValue. Both options are written up in README.md. OTHER DELIBERATE REFUSALS TO GUESS: only the 7 AC-named suffixes are variants -- the tree carries ~40 other trailing tokens and they are NOT all variants (Belphemon_Sleep, Choasmon_ValdurArm are forms), so unlisted tokens stay in displayName and are printed under 'unrecognized trailing tokens' for a human. A stage suffix is stripped ONLY when it names the folder the sprite is actually in, so it can never be wrong about a stage (the folder is the authority); _Digitama is never stripped since it is part of the name and the seed spells it 'Agu Digitama' -- the generator reproduces that seed displayName exactly. Digitama->Child: 47 of 57 matched by checking candidates against files that exist (prefix+mon, prefix+-mon for V->V-mon, and a variant baked into the prefix for Agu2006->Agumon_2006 / GabuBlack->Gabumon_Black); the 10 unmatched are printed and were each verified genuinely unmatchable -- Betamon/Kamemon/Kudamon/Kudamon_2006/Zubamon/PawnChessmon_Black/_White are idle-only with no animated Child sheet, Espimon and Liollmon exist nowhere in the tree, and Meicoo's child form is filed as 'MeicooChild' with no underscore so no suffix rule can see it. The match authors NO edge: an egg's edge points at a Baby I, and which Baby I is derivable from nothing.

userStories.title: Add HealthKit authorization flow
userStories.description: As a user, I want to grant health access on first launch so the app can feed my Digimon.
acceptanceCriteria: Requests read-only authorization for stepCount, activeEnergyBurned, sleepAnalysis, appleExerciseTime
Onboarding screen explains what is read and that data never leaves the watch, shown BEFORE the system prompt
Denial shows an explanatory state with guidance to Settings and does not crash or hang
Partial authorization is handled: unavailable types read zero rather than erroring
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)
Verify in watchOS Simulator (xcrun simctl io booted screenshot)

userStories.title: Query today's steps, calories and exercise
userStories.description: As a developer, I need current-day health values so they can be converted into energy.
acceptanceCriteria: Queries stepCount, activeEnergyBurned and appleExerciseTime for today from local-timezone midnight
Each value exposes a 'no data' state distinct from a real zero
Unit test with fixture samples asserts only today's samples are summed and yesterday's are excluded
Tests pass
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)

userStories.title: Query last night's sleep with windowing and dedup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need reliable sleep minutes so Spirit energy is accurate.
acceptanceCriteria: Queries sleepAnalysis in the window 18:00 previous day to 12:00 today and selects the longest asleep block
Sums only asleepCore, asleepDeep, asleepREM, asleepUnspecified; excludes inBed and awake
De-duplicates overlapping samples from multiple sources so time is never double-counted
Unit test asserts inBed samples are excluded from the total
Unit test asserts two overlapping sources produce the union, not the sum
Tests pass
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)

userStories.title: Convert health metrics to energy with delta crediting
userStories.description: As a user, I want my activity to become energy so my Digimon grows from what I actually did.
acceptanceCriteria: EnergyRates constants file defines: 1 Strength per 100 steps, 1 Vitality per 20 active kcal, 1 Spirit per 15 min asleep, 1 Stamina per 2 exercise minutes
Energy is credited only for the delta since the last read; reopening the app never double-credits
Daily cap of 100 points per energy type per day is enforced
Energy accrues to both stageEnergy and lifetimeEnergy
Unit test asserts reading twice with no new health data credits zero the second time
Unit test asserts the 100/day cap holds when a huge sample arrives
Tests pass
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)

userStories.title: Compute dominant energy type
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the dominant energy type so the evolution engine can pick a branch.
acceptanceCriteria: Dominant energy = the type with the highest stageEnergy total
Ties are broken by the most recently incremented type
Returns nil when all four totals are zero
Unit test covers a clear winner, a two-way tie broken by recency, and the all-zero case
Tests pass
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)

userStories.title: Build main screen with Digimon and stage info
userStories.description: As a user, I want to open the app and see my Digimon so I know how it is doing.
acceptanceCriteria: Digimon renders center-screen using DigimonSpriteView with the idle loop
Stage name and Digimon display name are shown
Refreshes health data when scenePhase becomes .active
Layout has no clipping at 41mm or 49mm
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)
Verify in watchOS Simulator at 41mm and 49mm (xcrun simctl io booted screenshot)

userStories.title: Add four energy bars to main screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my four energy types so I know what is feeding my Digimon.
acceptanceCriteria: Four bars or pip rows rendered with symbols: Strength=力, Vitality=活, Spirit=心, Stamina=耐
Each shows progress toward the current node's next evolution minEnergy
The dominant energy type is visually distinguished from the other three
Layout has no clipping at 41mm
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)
Verify in watchOS Simulator (xcrun simctl io booted screenshot)

userStories.title: Implement egg hatching
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to start with an egg that hatches from my activity so my first Digimon feels earned.
acceptanceCriteria: New game starts at a randomly selected Digitama node animating its 3 frames
Hatches when total energy across all four types reaches 50
Hatch resolves to the Baby I form linked to that Digitama in the graph
Hatched Digimon is written to persistence and recorded in the Dex
Unit test asserts 49 total energy does not hatch and 50 does
Tests pass
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)

userStories.title: Implement evolution edge evaluation
userStories.description: As a user, I want my Digimon to evolve based on which energy I earned most so my habits shape the outcome.
acceptanceCriteria: On each energy update the engine evaluates the current node's outgoing edges
An edge qualifies only if dominant energy matches requiredEnergy AND stageEnergy >= minEnergy AND careMistakes <= maxCareMistakes AND battleWins >= minBattleWins when set
When multiple edges qualify, the one with the highest minEnergy wins
Evolution resets stageEnergy to zero, preserves lifetimeEnergy, sets stageEnteredDate, and adds to the Dex
Unit test asserts strength-dominant and spirit-dominant states from the same node produce different results
Unit test asserts an edge is blocked when careMistakes exceeds maxCareMistakes
Tests pass
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)

userStories.title: Add evolution time gating and default fallback
userStories.description: As a user, I want stages to take real time so my Digimon cannot skip its whole life in one session.
acceptanceCriteria: Minimum time-in-stage enforced: 24h for Baby I and Baby II, 72h for Child and above
No evolution occurs before the time gate even when energy thresholds are met
When the time gate has passed and no edge qualifies, the node's isDefault edge is taken so a Digimon is never permanently stuck
Time thresholds live in one constants file
Clock is injectable so tests do not wait real hours
Unit test with a mock clock asserts no evolution at 23h and evolution at 25h for a Baby I
Unit test asserts the default edge fires when no edge qualifies after the gate
Tests pass
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)

userStories.title: Add evolution animation and haptic
userStories.description: As a user, I want a moment of ceremony when my Digimon evolves so the payoff feels real.
acceptanceCriteria: Full-screen animation plays: old sprite, flash or silhouette, new sprite
WKInterfaceDevice haptic .success fires at the reveal
New Digimon name is announced on screen
Animation triggers on app open if the evolution happened while the app was closed
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds for the watchOS target)
Verify in watchOS Simulator (xcrun simctl io booted screenshot)

userStories.title: Build Digimon Dex screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see every Digimon I have raised so I have a reason to try different habits.
acceptanceCriteria: Scrollable LazyVGrid of discovered Digimon using 'Idle Frame Only' 16x16 sprites

Tapping an entry shows name, stage, and date first obtained

Images load lazily; the full roster is never decoded eagerly

userStories.title: Add hunger that increases over real time

acceptanceCriteria: Hunger increases one unit per 4 hours of elapsed real time, computed from the last-updated timestamp
Hunger is capped at a max value defined in the constants file
Hunger is correct after the app has been closed for days (computed from elapsed time, not ticks)
Unit test with a mock clock asserts 12 elapsed hours adds exactly 3 hunger units

acceptanceCriteria: Feed button costs Vitality energy and reduces hunger by one unit
